text.title: django-base-model Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for django-base-model as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Switching PK to UUID retroactively — pretends to be a 5-line change, is actually destructive.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reject any migration that drops/recreates id. Always go via separate uid column with backfill.

failure-mode.description: Agents add unique=True and forget partial-index requirement when soft-delete is enabled.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leads to IntegrityError after restore. Replace every unique=True on a soft-deletable model with UniqueConstraint(condition=Q(deleted_at__isnull=True)).

failure-mode.description: Default objects shadowing. Agents put objects = SoftDeleteManager() first and lose access to deleted rows everywhere — including admin and loaddata.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always preserve all_objects and update admin to use it.

failure-mode.description: CASCADE through soft-delete silently hard-deletes children.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Force a checklist: list every reverse relation and decide CASCADE/PROTECT/SET_NULL per edge.

failure-mode.description: Tenant context leaks in Celery tasks because get_current_tenant() reads thread-local set by middleware.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents that "just call Model.objects.all()" return cross-tenant rows. Force explicit context with tenant_context(tenant).

failure-mode.description: db_default=Now() confused with auto_now_add=True. They behave differently for bulk_create, update, raw SQL.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pick one and document in the base model.

failure-mode.description: GeneratedField(db_persist=True) generates SQL that errors on SQLite.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents that ship SQLite-targeted dev DBs will see migration errors only in CI. Test on the prod DB engine.

failure-mode.description: HistoricalRecords(inherit=True) combined with abstract base + 100 child models silently creates 100 extra tables.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Reviewers should always count migrations. Use HistoricalRecords selectively on compliance-required models only.

failure-mode.description: .delete() semantic flip. Once SoftDeleteMixin is in, Model.objects.filter(...).delete() no longer hits the row-level override (Django QuerySet delete() bypasses it).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents must override the QuerySet too. Use SoftDeleteQuerySet that overrides delete() at the queryset level.

failure-mode.description: objects.update() bypasses signals and save() — soft-delete logic that lives in delete() won't run.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents who write "set deleted_at via update" miss that the queryset path needs its own override.

failure-mode.description: uid as URL param without a length-validating regex lets /users/<uid:uuid>/ accept any string.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use Django's uuid path converter, not <str:uid>.

failure-mode.description: Mass-rename of id references to uid in templates/serializers without migration creates dead URL routes.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Migrate views + URL conf in the same PR as the model change.
